name: First Security Bank
description_short: Community bank operating 12 branches across Minnesota, North Dakota, and Iowa with FDIC insurance, offering personal, business, and agricultural banking services.
description_long:
First Security Banks is a regional community bank with roots in the Upper Midwest, operating since at least 1878 in some locations. The bank maintains 12 branch locations across three states: Minnesota (Sleepy Eye, Benson, Storden, Renville, Lamberton, Canby, Lake Benton), North Dakota (Beulah, Robinson), and Iowa (Mapleton, Danbury). The organization is FDIC-insured and emphasizes local decision-making and personalized service through relationships with customers.

The bank offers comprehensive financial services across three main segments: Personal Banking (checking accounts, savings accounts, online banking, mobile banking, debit cards, and ID theft protection), Business Banking (business accounts, loans, and capital equipment financing), and Agriculture Banking (farm loans, crop insurance available in Iowa only, and farm management services). Additional services include trust services (available through First Security Bank – Mapleton since 1878), bill pay, vehicle loans, mortgages, and leasing options. Their marketing emphasizes competitive rates and low fees.

First Security Banks distinguishes itself through deep agricultural expertise and relationships in rural communities, offering specialized products like crop insurance and farm management services not typically available at larger institutions. The bank's owner, L.A. Amundson, established a scholarship program in 1993 with a $5 million endowment; as of 2025, the L.A. Amundson Ohana Foundation has awarded over 6,000 scholarships totaling $8.5 million. The bank markets itself as offering "true banker" advice with real-world understanding of local markets and agricultural capital needs.

However, First Security Banks operates as four separate chartered banks (First Security Bank – Sleepy Eye, First Security Bank – West, First Security Bank – Canby, and First Security Bank – Mapleton), meaning FDIC insurance coverage is limited to $250,000 per depositor per bank rather than across the entire organization. Geographic reach is limited to a three-state region in the Upper Midwest, making this impractical for customers outside this area. The website lacks transparency on specific rates, fees, or detailed product terms. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Debit cards with fraud protection and replacement services
Business loans and capital equipment financing/leasing
Agricultural loans and crop insurance (Iowa only)
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit of U.S. Government backing
Specialized agricultural banking with crop insurance and farm management services (Iowa locations)
Trust services available through First Security Bank – Mapleton since 1878
Strong community commitment demonstrated by $8.5 million in scholarships awarded through L.A. Amundson Ohana Foundation
Mobile and online banking available alongside 12 in-person branch locations
Competitive rates and low fees emphasized as core value proposition
Personal relationship-based banking model with local decision-making authority
cons: FDIC coverage capped at $250,000 per depositor per separate bank charter, not unified across all locations
Limited geographic footprint restricted to Minnesota, North Dakota, and Iowa only
Website lacks specific APR rates, fee schedules, or detailed product terms for comparison shopping
Trust services only available at Mapleton location, not all branches
Crop insurance and farm management services limited to Iowa locations only
best_for: Agricultural businesses and farmers in Minnesota, North Dakota, and Iowa seeking specialized farm lending and crop insurance
Small business owners in rural Upper Midwest communities valuing local decision-making and personalized service
Residents of the three-state service area seeking community-focused banking with strong local ties
Customers prioritizing in-person relationship banking over digital-first services
diagnosis: First Security Banks is best for agricultural businesses, small business owners, and consumers in rural Minnesota, North Dakota, and Iowa who value personalized, relationship-based banking from a locally-owned community institution. The main caveat is that deposits are split across four separate FDIC-insured banks (limiting individual coverage to $250,000 per charter), the geographic footprint excludes most of the U.S., and the website provides no pricing transparency to compare competitiveness against regional or national alternatives.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
